name: Camino del Cid: The Exile Trail
name_original: Ciruelos de Cervera desde Santo Domingo de Silos — Parque Natural Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla — circular por el Camino del Cid Cicloturista
description: Cycle a challenging 22.9-mile loop through Sabinares de Arlanza Natural Park, La Yecla Gorge, and Santo Domingo de Silos.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Santo Domingo De Silos traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, with significant elevation changes. The region features a mix of open terrain and forested sections, offering varied cycling environments. Routes often include climbs and descents, providing a challenging experience for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Santo Domingo De Silos The most popular touring cycling route is Camino del Cid: The Exile Trail, a 22.9 miles (36.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route follows a historic path, offering a challenging ride through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Sad Hill Cemetery – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Sad Hill, a difficult 45.5 miles (73.3 km) path. This route features extensive climbs and descents, passing through open country with views towards Peña de Carazo. Local touring cyclists also love the Sad Hill Cemetery – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Santo Domingo de Silos, a 37.2 miles (59.9 km) trail leading through hilly terrain and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 5 hours 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Santo Domingo De Silos is defined by significant elevation changes, open landscapes, and views of local hills like Peña de Carazo. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists seeking challenging routes. The routes in Santo Domingo De Silos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 280 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santo Domingo De Silos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Santo Domingo De Silos?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes documented on komoot for the Santo Domingo De Silos area, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners or families?
A: The touring cycling routes around Santo Domingo De Silos are primarily challenging, with 11 out of 12 routes rated as difficult. There is one moderate route, but no easy routes are currently listed, making the area more suitable for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Santo Domingo De Silos?
A: The region is characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, featuring significant elevation changes. You'll encounter a mix of open terrain and forested sections, with routes often including substantial climbs and descents. Views towards local hills like Peña de Carazo are common.

Q: What are some of the longer touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For a longer ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Sad Hill&lt;/a&gt;, which is 45.5 miles (73.3 km) long and features extensive climbs. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19882193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Santo Domingo de Silos&lt;/a&gt;, covering 37.2 miles (59.9 km) through hilly terrain.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Santo Domingo De Silos?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Sad Hill&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19882193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Santo Domingo de Silos&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options, offering varied landscapes and views.

Q: What interesting places or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant landmarks. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4997641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña de Carazo&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2521846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Santo Domingo de Silos&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5458997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Doña Sancha&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4659990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Santo Domingo De Silos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Santo Domingo De Silos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging nature of the routes, the varied landscapes, and the scenic views, particularly towards Peña de Carazo.

Q: What is the typical duration for a touring cycling route in this region?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on length and elevation. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030257994/camino-del-cid-the-exile-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid: The Exile Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 21 minutes, while longer loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19882193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Santo Domingo de Silos&lt;/a&gt; can take closer to 5 hours 52 minutes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of mountain peaks or summits?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent views of the surrounding peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña de Carazo loop from Santo Domingo de Silos&lt;/a&gt; offers direct views of Peña de Carazo. You might also encounter highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5283553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro San Cristóbal&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7170777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Castro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Santo Domingo De Silos?
A: Given the region's rolling hills and open landscapes, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Alto de Peña Alta area?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Peña Alta (1078 m) – Casa de Doña Sancha loop from Santo Domingo de Silos&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3568119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Peña Alta (1078 m)&lt;/a&gt;, offering a challenging ride with significant elevation changes.
tips: The Monasterio de Santo Domingo de Silos is considered to be one of the most culturally and historically significant monasteries in Spain. If you are lucky, during a visit to the monastery you will be able to enjoy the world-famous Gregorian chants of the monks, who still cultivate and practice them today as ever. An album released by the monks was in the US charts for 53 weeks and went platinum three times.

A small town next to the Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Area. It is worth seeing its church of San Sebastián Mártir from the 13th century, the hermitage of Carmen, an old laundry room and the fountain in the Plaza Mayor.

The La Yecla gorge is a deep and narrow gorge excavated in the limestone banks so characteristic of the relief of the Peñas de Cervera, enabled to make a route through a series of footbridges. It is an easy and very attractive walk of a kilometer and a half.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular touring cycling route, covering approximately 36.8 kilometers, is estimated to take around 2 hours and 21 minutes to complete. This duration is for cycling time and does not include stops for sightseeing or breaks.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this route as 'difficult' for touring bicycles. While the Camino del Cid Cicloturista, which this route is part of, is generally described as 'low-medium' difficulty, the specific elevation gain of around 467 meters and the general altitude of the Sabinares de Arlanza Natural Park (around 1000 meters) suggest it's best suited for cyclists with some experience. Hybrid or trekking bikes are ideal for the varied terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the historic town of Santo Domingo de Silos, which is a key starting point and a major highlight along the route. Parking is also available near other settlements like Ciruelos de Cervera or close to the La Yecla Gorge area.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows paved secondary roads with low traffic, and some regional or national roads, making it well-suited for touring bikes. A notable section is the BU-911, a 'Carretera Singular' for cyclists. While generally accessible, expect some climbs due to the park's altitude, with a gentle downhill section towards Espinosa de Cervera after the La Yecla Gorge.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several significant points of interest. These include the historic Monasterio de Santo Domingo de Silos, the charming settlement of Ciruelos de Cervera, and the dramatic Viewpoint over the Yecla Gorge. The gorge itself, with its metallic walkways, offers a unique geological experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route immerses you in the stunning landscapes of the Sabinares de Arlanza Natural Park, home to the world's largest and best-preserved juniper forests. You'll also see holm oak, Pyrenean oak, pine, and riverside forests along the Arlanza River. The dramatic limestone cliffs and narrow canyon of La Yecla Gorge are a major scenic highlight.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the region's climate and the nature of touring cycling, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the best times. The weather is usually milder, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, and winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for adverse weather conditions.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I see in the Sabinares de Arlanza Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The park is a haven for wildlife, particularly birds of prey. You're very likely to spot a significant colony of griffon vultures soaring above the La Yecla Gorge. The diverse forest ecosystems also support various other bird species and mammals, though they might be more elusive.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the larger Camino del Cid Cicloturista, specifically the 'El Destierro' section. It also intersects with other official trails such as the Camino de Santiago de la Lana.BTT, Camino de Santiago de la Lana.Senderista, GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, and the Camino del Cid (Senderista).

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sabinares de Arlanza Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Always check specific regulations for the natural park and any protected areas you might pass through, as rules can vary.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for the Sabinares de Arlanza Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for general access to the Sabinares de Arlanza Natural Park for activities like cycling. However, always respect park regulations, especially concerning wildlife, waste, and fire prevention. For specific details, you can consult official tourism resources like spain.info.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Santo Domingo de Silos is a historic town with amenities including accommodation, cafes, and restaurants. Smaller villages like Ciruelos de Cervera may offer more limited services, but you'll find options for refreshments and lodging in the larger settlements along or near the Camino del Cid route.

waypoints.name: Monasterio de Santo Domingo de Silos
